product.title: Greenies Supplements Calming Chews for Dogs Chicken Flavor, Dog Calming Chews, 7.3 oz. Container of 40 Count
product.description: These Greenies calming dog treats from the makers of vet-recommended Greenies Dental Treats let pet parents and dog owners offer gentle support to their furry friends. This L Theanine supplement is blended with L-Tryptophan to foster calmness and relaxation while helping support positive behavior during normal or occasional stress. These calming supplements for dogs come in chewy bites made with real chicken and contain no artificial flavors or colors, and no fillers. It is used for behavior management. Made in the USA with quality ingredients sourced from around the world and formulated by PH.D animal nutritionists, the supplement carries the National Animal Supplement Council Quality Seal, giving dog parents and pet owners confidence in every bite.
feature_bullets: Supports Calmness and Relaxation: Greenies Dog Calm Chews Supplements are formulated with L-Tryptophan and L-Theanine to support calmness and relaxation
Calming Support: These dog calming treats support positive behavior during times of normal and occasional stress
Made With Real Chicken: Greenies calming treats for dogs are supplements made with real chicken for irresistible taste and no artificial flavors or colors, and no fillers

local.title: Work so good. HIGHLY RECOMMEND for overly hyperactive dogs.
local.text: These calmed my dog down so much I thought she had died. She usually starts whining and going crazy the moment my feet hit the floor in the morning and she didnt make a peep. I honestly thought I might go down to find her dead bc that has NEVER happened before. Nope she wasnt dead, just laying there waiting patiently like a good girl. I was even able to actually pet her which is usually impossible bc shes so hyper and crazy.
local.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 7, 2026

local.title: They work!
local.text: I don’t write reviews, but these treats are great! I have a 1 year old boxer who has terrible separation anxiety. We had to re-kennel train him because he tore up the house one day from free roaming. Ever since, when we would leave, he would cry, and howl, it was so loud I could hear it outside my apartment complex. We’d leave the tv on, music, kongs, lick mats, none of it helped. So I decided to give these a try. Works like a charm. I give him the desired amount an hour before I leave and he’s been sleeping for hours! I think these help a ton!
local.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 29, 2026

local.text: Amazing product, I have a dog with high anxiety.. within an hour of taking these calming, choose he was completely chilled out and out like a light!
local.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 27, 2026

local.title: I give them a 4 out of 5
local.text: I have a very anxious puppy mill survivor. He shakes all the time at any new, sudden noise. He freaks out for grooming appointments and car rides. These work pretty well for him with no side effects. He has no teeth due to the hideous conditions at the puppy mill where he was kept prisoner. The treats are soft and easily crushed into almost like a power substance that he can easily manage. I wrote a kid's chapter book about our experience living with a puppy mill survivor. It's called Red Alert Ruby, available here on Amazon. Adopt don't shop!
local.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 23, 2026

local.title: My veterinarian approved this for my dog
local.text: Hey People, OK so my bagel has been having a lot of anxiety and he is 22 months old-it had recently just started when my neighbor got into a fight and yelling and slamming doors-so now every time any of my neighbors go in and out of their apartment, hunters, anxiety rises-and it’s not just minor where you can redirect him. It gets to the extreme.— so I contacted my veterinarian, and I talked to them about solutions because I didn’t want to put him on medicine that he would have to be on for the rest of his life which when a dog goes on an anxiety medicine they have to be on it for the rest of our life and I didn’t want this kind of life For Hunter-so I had found these and before ordering them I talked it over with my Vet veterinarian office and they talked it over with his vet veterinarian who also has four beagles— they did their research and they told me to go ahead and do these that it was safe for him.. now Hunter is 30 pounds and it does suggest that you can do too well for the first time and only time so far I only did one cause I didn’t want to overdo it with him— and within about 15/20 minutes it actually kicked in for him and he was actually chilled all day and most of the night— he’s usually pretty quiet dog in our apartment.. but since the anxiety the past couple weeks, I had to do something.. So I’m glad that I ordered these, and I will only give them to him only as needed for extreme measures-like a thunderstorm if they start shaking or anything like he does sometimes not all the time— fireworks aren’t allowed in the city, but you do hear them sometimes— so I do not plan to give these to him all the time because I don’t want my dog to be medicated only as needed.. because no matter how good medicine is keep in mind all medicine is just like with humans. It can mess up their liver and stuff so don’t use these as a long-term in to get out of your dog being rambunctious because dogs are dogs.-just use these for anxiety and only as needed.. thunderstorm/fireworks and if something set your baby off to get shaking and barking constantly.. But I will suggest that you screenshot the picture and mention the name and talk to your veterinarian before giving to your dog-because even though we know our dogs best because we’re with them all the time your veterinarian knows they’re overall health— and what each breed can handle.. that’s why I contacted my veterinarian so they can do researching on it and check all the ingredients and everything before giving it to Hunter because I will always look out for him.. I don’t give him just any kind of human food because that all human foods are good for him-and I Google everything-even when I read something if it says it’s OK in a small portions but it’s not recommended. I take that as a no and not as OK small portion let me do it… So please, before giving this to your dog, talk to your veterinarian about it first.. and if it says they can take two start up with one— if it says you can give three or four start out with one write the time down and what time you gave it to your baby and monitor your baby… be safe and sorry anytime you’ve given your pet anything especially medicine even if it is OTC.. not everything is good for your pet… Thank you for reading this I will definitely buy again and now that I know how my dog reacts to just taken one and it helps. I will not buy anything else or experiment with any other kind…My friends all told me that I can get CBD dog treats.. but that’s just a drug going into your dog… and we all know that Pot isn’t good for us-so then why would you give it to your pet?-yes I know it’s a different strain.— but really think about it.. Do your pet right!!!! Always consult with your vet veterinarian This product is veterinarian approved with our vet here in Maine for my breed of dog(beagle) and at the same time my beagle doesn’t have any other health issues.. now I think if my beagle had any other health issues, this would view would be different or it wouldn’t be a review because if my vet said not to do it, I would not have bought it.. Thank you for reading this…
local.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 11, 2026

local.title: No calming for my pup but a valued treat
local.text: Not sure they do anything to calm the pup down but she likes them. I probably won't get them again, just sub with a high value treat.
local.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 24, 2026

local.title: Reluctant, but these do work!!
local.text: I never write reviews but these really worked for us. We recently adopted our dog who was previously abandoned and she has some separation anxiety. I gave her one the first day while crate training and it may have calmed her down a little but I wasn’t totally sure. A few days later I gave her 2 (the correct dose) and she has been asleep in her crate for an hour which is huge! I don’t plan on giving these every day but to know they work as a backup if I have to leave is really great. Also wanted to add she does not have severe separation anxiety because we’ve never truly left her yet, so for mild/moderate separation anxiety this works great. I also really appreciate that these don’t have a sedative in them, just calming ingredients that don’t completely knock her out.
local.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 12, 2026

local.title: He let me brush his face!
local.text: These really work. I have a 13lb Shih Tzu who doesn’t like his face being brushed. I gave him one of these and he loved the chicken flavor, after 30 mins or so he was very calm. He is a calm dog bug he gets anxious when it comes to brushing, but after using he let me brush his face and his was very relaxed fun the experience. Hopefully he’ll have the same calmness at the groomers.
local.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 11, 2026
